title: IntentLock: A Deterministic Intent-to-Commit Control and Evidence Architecture for AI-Assisted Software Development
description:
Problem. AI coding agents can make broad, technically valid changes that exceed what a human intended to authorize. Sandboxes and PR review help, but the producer of a change can still blur into the authority that admits it.

Gap. Existing tools offer containment, review, or build attestations separately. Few bind a signed human task to a Diff Envelope, forbid agent self-acceptance, and emit an offline-verifiable intent-to-commit proof capsule.

Proposed solution. IntentLock converts a human task into a signed Intent Contract, compiles Diff Envelope and capability policy, records non-authoritative execution units, freezes candidates, and admits changes only at an independent boundary with fail-closed verdicts.

Key evidence (v6.0.0). Importable library from intentlock import IntentLockEngine; Reality Gate 8/8; benchmarks; mutation testing ≥90%; alternative-implementation replication; real-world README-vs-CI expansion scenario; stress to 2000 execution units.

Honest limits. Not production, not peer reviewed, not independently replicated, not universal agent security. Concept DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.21361708. Released for evaluation, citation, and attempted falsification.
notes: Independent Research Publication No. 3. v6.0.0. Concept DOI 10.5281/zenodo.21361708. PDF REGENERATION NEEDED. Not peer reviewed. Not production. Generative AI tools assisted preparation. Author responsible for content.
